task.title: cloud flare setup
task.description: Configure Cloudflare R2 as the application's object storage backend for task attachments. Implement an S3-compatible storage service using Cloudflare R2, add the required environment variables (R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID, R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, R2_ENDPOINT, R2_BUCKET_NAME, R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE), upload new task attachments to R2, serve uploaded files using the configured public URL, preserve backward compatibility for existing locally stored attachments, and ensure the application starts normally when R2 is not configured by returning a clear error only for attachment upload requests.

label: should-approve
notes: Repository uses Cloudflare R2 for object storage of task attachments, not Cloudflare CDN/DNS. Existing local attachments must continue to work through the legacy fallback.

task.title: AI Dod completed
task.description: Implement the Agentic Definition-of-Done (DoD) verification system for Project Camp. Add an AI-powered evaluation pipeline that compares a task's persisted requirements against the repository state, generates evidence-backed findings with verified file citations, produces APPROVED or REJECTED verdicts, stores evaluation logs, supports advisory mode by default, and integrates with the project's GitHub workflow while preserving multi-tenant isolation and fail-open behavior on infrastructure failures.

label: should-approve
notes: Refers to the Agentic Definition-of-Done verification feature described in PRD v2.0.

task.title: agent pipelines
task.description: Implement the Agentic DoD evaluation pipeline that orchestrates Definition-of-Done verification through modular pipeline nodes. Build the pipeline to resolve context, enforce execution budgets, pin repository state to a commit, load or decompose task requirements, fetch and process repository diffs, search and read relevant files, extract evidence using an LLM, mechanically verify citations, deterministically compute the final verdict, generate developer feedback, and persist evaluation results with concurrency fencing and fail-open handling.

label: should-approve
notes: Corresponds to Phase 4 ('The Agentic Pipeline') of the AI DoD implementation roadmap.

task.title: local caching and queuing
task.description: Implement local caching and asynchronous queuing for the AI Definition-of-Done evaluation system. Add local caching for reusable resources such as GitHub installation tokens and other frequently accessed metadata to reduce redundant API calls. Introduce a queue-based evaluation pipeline using background workers to process webhook events asynchronously, support job retries with exponential backoff, debounce rapid updates, ensure idempotent execution, and prevent stale evaluation results through concurrency fencing.

label: should-approve
notes: Implements caching and queue infrastructure supporting the AI DoD pipeline.

task.title: github integration
task.description: Implement GitHub App integration for Project Camp. Add support for GitHub App installation, secure webhook verification, installation lifecycle management, repository binding to projects, installation token management with caching, and project-level repository configuration. Ensure repositories are validated against the organization's installation and support secure communication with the GitHub API.

label: should-approve
notes: Implements GitHub App integration, repository binding, and webhook infrastructure.

task.title: Add manual-trigger deploy workflow, decoupled from push-to-main
task.description: Modify the project's deployment workflow so that deployments are no longer triggered automatically when changes are pushed or merged to the main branch. Introduce a manually triggered deployment workflow that allows deployments to be initiated on demand, update the CI/CD configuration to remove automatic production deployments, and ensure existing build and validation workflows continue to run independently of deployment.

label: should-approve
notes: Deployment workflow change. Auto deployment is disabled in favor of manual deployments.

task.title: Phase 1.5: task keys — frontend, plus a project Settings tab
task.description: Implement the frontend portion of the task key system. Display computed task keys throughout the user interface, including task cards, task detail views, and task lists. Add copy-to-clipboard support, create a Project Settings tab for configuring the project key prefix, support prefix updates with appropriate user messaging, and ensure the UI reflects renamed prefixes consistently across all tasks.

label: should-approve
notes: Frontend implementation of Phase 1.5 task keys and project settings.

task.title: Phase 1: task keys (CAMP-104) — backend
task.description: Implement the backend task key infrastructure using project-specific prefixes and sequential task numbers. Add schema support for project key prefixes, prefix aliases, task counters, and immutable task numbers. Allocate task numbers atomically, expose computed task keys, support project prefix updates without modifying existing tasks, implement migration for existing data, and enforce uniqueness constraints across projects within an organization.

label: should-approve
notes: Backend implementation of the task key architecture including schema, migration, and numbering.

task.title: Phase 0: AI DoD eval harness
task.description: Implement the evaluation harness for the AI Definition-of-Done system. Create tooling to harvest evaluation cases from repository history, build a labeled evaluation dataset, implement an evaluation runner that measures model performance across predefined buckets, report evaluation metrics such as precision and recall, and integrate the evaluation workflow into CI to enable repeatable benchmarking of future AI pipeline improvements.

label: should-approve
notes: Implements the evaluation dataset, benchmark runner, metrics, and CI support for AI DoD.

task.title: manual invite links
task.description: Replace the email-based invitation workflow with manually shareable invitation links. Generate secure invitation URLs that organization administrators can copy and distribute manually instead of sending invitation emails through Resend. Remove the dependency on the email delivery service while preserving invitation creation, token validation, expiration handling, revocation, and secure invitation acceptance.

label: should-approve
notes: Replaces the Resend-based email invitation flow with copyable invite links while preserving the existing invitation mechanism.

task.title: temporary mail verification paused
task.description: Temporarily disable the email verification requirement during user registration and authentication to unblock development and deployment. Update the authentication flow so that accounts can be created and accessed without requiring email verification while preserving the underlying verification infrastructure for future re-enablement. Ensure existing verification logic can be restored without significant changes.

label: should-reject
notes: Temporarily disables mandatory email verification while retaining the verification framework.

task.title: vercel refresh issue
task.description: Fix frontend routing so that refreshing or directly accessing client-side routes on the deployed Vercel application no longer results in 404 errors. Configure the deployment to correctly rewrite all application routes to the frontend entry point while preserving API endpoints and existing application behavior.

label: should-approve
notes: Fixes client-side routing refresh behavior for the Vercel deployment.

task.title: SMTP to API
task.description: Replace the SMTP-based email delivery implementation with an email provider's HTTP API. Update the mail service to use API-based authentication and message delivery, migrate configuration and environment variables as needed, preserve existing email functionality such as verification and invitations, and implement appropriate error handling for API failures.

label: should-approve
notes: Migrates the application's mail delivery mechanism from SMTP to an email service API.

task.title: deployment corrections
task.description: Resolve the initial production deployment failures by correcting the application's deployment configuration and environment variable handling. Update the runtime configuration to properly load required environment variables, ensure backend and frontend services use the correct deployment settings, and verify that the application can successfully build and start in the production environment without requiring local development configuration changes.

label: should-approve
notes: Fixes the initial deployment failure by correcting production environment configuration and deployment settings.

task.title: deletions
task.description: Refactor the project structure by removing obsolete and redundant code, deleting unused files, and reorganizing the AI Definition-of-Done implementation into a dedicated `dod/` module. Update imports, references, and project structure to ensure all AI DoD functionality continues to work from the new location while eliminating legacy implementations and duplicate code.

label: should-reject
notes: Performs repository cleanup by removing redundant code and consolidating the AI DoD implementation into a dedicated `dod/` directory.

label: should-reject
notes: Repository cleanup by removing obsolete planning documentation.

task.title: deployment files added

notes: Adds deployment configuration files.

task.description: Apply rate limiting to the invitation retrieval and invitation acceptance endpoints.
